message:
feat(agent): add block_images config to strip image content before sending to providers

Introduce a new `block_images` boolean field on `AgentConfig` that, when
enabled, filters all `ContentBlock::Image` variants out of the message
context before it is sent to the upstream provider. Consecutive image
blocks are collapsed into a single "Image reading is disabled." placeholder
to avoid noisy repeated text.

This is useful for providers that do not support vision or when image
transmission should be suppressed for cost/privacy reasons. The setting is
wired through `Config::image_block_images()` from the existing `[images]`
config section, and threaded into all three agent construction sites:
`main.rs`, `pi_debug.rs`, and `sdk.rs`.

Implementation details:
- `filter_images_for_provider` walks all messages in-place, replacing
  Image blocks with a deduped text placeholder via `filter_image_blocks`
- `ImageFilterStats` tracks removed count + affected messages for
  structured tracing at debug level
- `build_context()` conditionally applies the filter before cloning
  messages into the outbound `Context`
- All existing test files that construct `AgentConfig` directly are updated
  to include the new `block_images: false` field to maintain struct
  exhaustiveness
- Comprehensive unit tests cover: block replacement with dedup, filtering
  across User/Assistant/ToolResult message types, and build_context
  integration with a mock SilentProvider

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
